claims.summary: The 768D-to-8D embedding projection is a compression bottleneck and should keep reporting raw-intensity/detail loss while live delivery remains bounded.
Entropy can overstate noisy variety or understate structured intentionality; lexical and structural diagnostics should distinguish richness from chaos.
Using reserved dims 44-47 for projection expansion could reduce dead-zone pressure, but it would change live semantic transport and needs explicit operator approval.
Semantic-collision risk should be tested by checking whether distinct dense embedding inputs remain distinguishable in the existing 8D projection lane.
